name: Histamine burden tendency
reviewNotes: Starter-reviewed for release as an informational pathway tendency model. The model has explicit claim membership, pathway-axis text, contribution states, coverage thresholds, and limitations.
knownWeaknesses: Generated from curated starter-panel pathway assignments and not yet expert-reviewed per variant mechanism.
Axis mapping is starter-level and may need pathway-specific refinement before stronger claims.
Direct rsID matching is used; genome build and strand normalization are not yet fail-closed.
Measured biomarkers, medications, diet, symptoms, and clinical context are not included.
releaseUse: Informational genetic pathway tendency scoring only; not diagnostic, clinical, treatment, or population-percentile interpretation.
biologicalQuestion: Do the curated SNP findings favor lower or higher modelled histamine burden?

inputs.inclusionRationale: Included because the starter SNP panel assigns AOC1 rs1049793 to Histamine with explicit genotype contribution components.

limitations: This is a relative pathway tendency within the MetaboBrief starter model, not a population percentile.
This model combines curated SNP contribution components assigned to this pathway; it does not measure current pathway activity.
The current browser analyzer still performs direct provider-native rsID matching without build or strand normalization.
Not included: Measured histamine, dietary histamine, mast-cell diagnosis, medications, current symptoms.
